verses.text: False prophets shall arise, 'who privily shall bring in damnable heresies,' even denying the Lord; their judgment lingers not. Beware. Deceivers will rise and meet sure ruin.
False prophets shall arise, 'who privily shall bring in damnable heresies,' even denying the Lord; their judgment lingers not. Beware. Deceivers will rise and meet sure ruin.
False prophets shall arise, 'who privily shall bring in damnable heresies,' even denying the Lord; their judgment lingers not. Beware. Deceivers will rise and meet sure ruin.
God spared not the sinning angels, nor the old world, nor Sodom, made 'an ensample unto those that... live ungodly.' Past judgments. History warns the ungodly of certain wrath.
God spared not the sinning angels, nor the old world, nor Sodom, made 'an ensample unto those that... live ungodly.' Past judgments. History warns the ungodly of certain wrath.
God spared not the sinning angels, nor the old world, nor Sodom, made 'an ensample unto those that... live ungodly.' Past judgments. History warns the ungodly of certain wrath.

He delivered righteous Lot, vexed by the wicked; 'the Lord knoweth how to deliver the godly... and to reserve the unjust' for judgment. Rescue and reserve. God saves His own and holds the wicked for judgment.

He delivered righteous Lot, vexed by the wicked; 'the Lord knoweth how to deliver the godly... and to reserve the unjust' for judgment. Rescue and reserve. God saves His own and holds the wicked for judgment.

He delivered righteous Lot, vexed by the wicked; 'the Lord knoweth how to deliver the godly... and to reserve the unjust' for judgment. Rescue and reserve. God saves His own and holds the wicked for judgment.
These presumptuous ones despise authority, speak evil of dignities, 'as natural brute beasts,' and 'shall utterly perish.' Brutish. Their reckless contempt leads to destruction.
These presumptuous ones despise authority, speak evil of dignities, 'as natural brute beasts,' and 'shall utterly perish.' Brutish. Their reckless contempt leads to destruction.
These presumptuous ones despise authority, speak evil of dignities, 'as natural brute beasts,' and 'shall utterly perish.' Brutish. Their reckless contempt leads to destruction.
They sport in their deceivings, 'having eyes full of adultery,' following Balaam, 'who loved the wages of unrighteousness.' Greedy way. Like Balaam, they sell themselves for gain.

They sport in their deceivings, 'having eyes full of adultery,' following Balaam, 'who loved the wages of unrighteousness.' Greedy way. Like Balaam, they sell themselves for gain.
They are 'wells without water,' promising liberty while themselves 'the servants of corruption.' Empty boasters. False teachers offer freedom but are slaves of sin.
They are 'wells without water,' promising liberty while themselves 'the servants of corruption.' Empty boasters. False teachers offer freedom but are slaves of sin.
They are 'wells without water,' promising liberty while themselves 'the servants of corruption.' Empty boasters. False teachers offer freedom but are slaves of sin.
Having escaped pollution, then entangled again, their last state is worse; 'the dog is turned to his own vomit again.' Worse than before. To turn back from the truth is dreadful.
